commands.review_summary:
This PR separates image runtime setup and provider request timeout handling, adds phase-specific timeout errors/tests, and clarifies image timeout docs.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main subtracts setup latency from the provider request timeout and clamps exhausted budgets to 1ms. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review, but the added fake-timer tests cover the source path directly.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; the PR is maintainer-owned/labeled and needs explicit maintainer approval of the timeout contract and upgrade-risk tradeoff.

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so the provider request keeps the full configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget
- bound image runtime setup with a separate internal 15 second watchdog instead of letting setup share or mirror the user-facing request timeout
- add focused tests covering slow setup, fixed setup timeout behavior, and the simplified timeout error messages
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media-understanding/image.test.ts`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout, and slow setup no longer stretches the user-facing request timeout into a setup budget.
Real environment tested: local source checkout via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` and Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file with the commands above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover a slow `400ms` setup that still gives the provider request `timeoutMs: 1000`, plus a separate setup timeout that now trips at the fixed internal `15000ms` watchdog.
Observed result after fix: the provider request still receives the full configured timeout after slow setup, setup failures abort before the request starts with a dedicated setup-timeout error, and request failures keep the simpler request-timeout message.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce image runtime-loading latency itself.
target.author: hxy91819
